mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat when GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth for three consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending and compresses equity valuations over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.5pp in a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicate an overheating economy facing aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and suppresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise month-over-month for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and business investment, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns remain negative despite a 3-month consecutive uptrend in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing retail sales volume indicates stronger consumer demand, which directly boosts quarterly earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where RSAFS shows sequential growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing starts above the 1200-unit threshold signal a robust real estate market, which stimulates construction activity and consumer wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 1200 units for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative when HOUST remains above 1200 units for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which reduces recession risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are negative when PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases simultaneously for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, which dampens economic activity and equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are positive when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% for three consecutive months.
